response.justification: Candidate has directly relevant recent experience at re:cinq building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, which aligns strongly with the AI-native engineering mindset the role demands. Distributed-systems credentials are solid (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handled millions of HTTP requests per second) and TypeScript depth is clear. The biggest gap is Python — listed as a must-have but absent from the stated skills and unmentioned across employment history. Worth a screen to verify Python proficiency and assess distributed-systems depth at modern scale before committing to a full loop.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent/MCP tooling experience at re:cinq matches the AI-native baseline
Proven high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Strong TDD, craftsmanship, and teaching background (workshops, dojos, courses)
concerns: Python — a must-have — is not listed in skills or referenced in any role
Recent product work skews to TypeScript/Node and DLang rather than Python ecosystems
No explicit evidence of on-call rotation, incident response, or large-scale operational ownership
